title: Easter biscuits
subtitle: Spiced currant biscuits from the West Country
excerpt: Crisp, lightly spiced biscuits with currants and lemon zest, traditional to the West Country at Easter. Not as heavily spiced as a fairing — more delicate, and rolled to a specific thinness. Finished with a glittering sugar crust.
sourceNotes: Florence White, Good Things in England (1932): Easter biscuits documented as a Gloucestershire and Somerset speciality. The recipe uses mixed spice, currants, and lemon zest in a short butter dough, traditionally eaten on Easter Sunday and at Eastertide fairs. Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136: rubbing-in method for a short biscuit dough.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze baked biscuits in a sealed box. Return to room temperature before eating.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps in a tin for a week.

glossaryTerms.definition: Small dried black grapes, distinct from fresh redcurrants and blackcurrants, which are entirely different plants. Currants are smaller and more intensely flavoured than raisins or sultanas, with a slight tartness. Traditional in British baking: hot cross buns, Eccles cakes, and Easter biscuits all use them.

content.text: Easter biscuits are lighter than a Cornish fairing: the spice is restrained, the dough shorter and more delicate.

sit whole in each biscuit rather than mixing in. The traditional West Country finish is an egg-white glaze brushed on halfway through baking, then dusted with caster sugar — it sets to a glistening, crystalline surface.

attrs.prepNote: large, beaten (keep the white for brushing)

content.text: Heat the oven to 180°C fan. Line two baking trays with baking paper.

Beat the {{unsalted-butter}} and {{caster-sugar}} together until pale. Beat in the egg yolk and the zest of the {{lemons}}. Mix in the {{plain-flour}}, {{mixed-spice}}, and {{fine-sea-salt}} until a dough forms. Stir in the {{currants}}. If the dough feels soft, refrigerate for 15 minutes.
Roll the dough on a lightly floured surface to 5 mm thick. Cut rounds with a 6 cm cutter. Place on the lined trays with 3 cm between them.

Bake for 5 minutes. Remove from the oven and brush the surfaces lightly with the reserved egg white. Sprinkle with a little extra {{caster-sugar}}. Return to the oven for a further 5–7 minutes until pale golden and the sugar has set to a gloss. Cool on a rack.
